description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a cylindrical base constructed from gray masonry blocks, giving it a robust and weathered appearance. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof, supported by two vertical wooden posts, and covered in reddish-brown shingles. A horizontal wooden beam spans the posts, holding a central spindle with a hand crank on one side, used for drawing water. The interior of the well is open, revealing a dark void where water would be accessed. The structure is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, and is designed to provide both shelter and access to water in an outdoor setting.
description: A stone water well with a pitched wooden roof, hand crank, and open cylindrical base, designed for outdoor water access.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hand crank, spindle, and the open well mouth beneath the pitched roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof and the cylindrical stone structure, with the crank handle not visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but the crank handle is on the opposite side, and the spindle is still visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the roof's profile and the stone well, with the crank handle not visible.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Cylindrical stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well opening; wooden roof supported by posts provides overhead protection.
